Configure which paths to scan for unused: Configure which paths to scan for unused datapoints. Each path will get its own result state with a JSON table.
Datapoints not updated for this many day: Datapoints not updated for this many days are marked as 'dead'
Datapoints not updated for this many days are marked as 'stale'
Each scan result contains a JSON array w: Each scan result contains a JSON array with the following fields per datapoint:
For German tables: Use status_de and iss:
For German tables: Use status_de and issue_de columns
For English tables: Use status and issue columns

Filter by 'issue != null' to show only problematic datapoints.
For alias.* paths: Check if target datap: For alias.* paths: Check if target datapoints exist (ghost detection)
How often the automatic scan should run: How often the automatic scan should run (minimum 1 hour)
When enabled, all configured paths will: When enabled, all configured paths will be scanned automatically at the specified interval
• name\n common.name or last part of ID:
• name
  common.name or last part of ID (user-friendly name)
• last_ts\n Unix timestamp in ms or nul:
• last_ts
  Unix timestamp in ms or null (for sorting)
• last_ts_iso\n Formatted date string (:
• last_ts_iso
  Formatted date string (for display in table)
• value\n Current datapoint value (fina:
• value
  Current datapoint value (final check before deletion)
• status\n Classification in English: a:
• status
  Classification in English: active, dead, stale, undefined, orphaned
• status_de\n Classification in German::
• status_de
  Classification in German: aktiv, inaktiv, veraltet, undefiniert, verwaist
• issue\n Problem indicator (English)::
• issue
  Problem indicator (English): dead, stale, orphaned_alias, or null if OK
• issue_de\n Problem indicator (German):
• issue_de
  Problem indicator (German): inaktiv, veraltet, verwaistes Alias, or null if OK
